story.iWant: automated Cloudflare Pages deployment that builds the WASM binary and deploys the web interface whenever code is pushed to the main branch
story.soThat: the live Recipe web app is always up-to-date with the latest code without manual deployment steps, and users access the latest features and bug fixes immediately
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create Cloudflare Pages Project (AC-3, AC-5) - Log in to Cloudflare Dashboard and create account if needed (free tier) - Create New Pages Project: Account Home → Workers & Pages → Pages → Create application - Connect GitHub Repository or use Direct Upload for manual deployment - Configure Build Settings: Project Name "recipe", Production Branch "main", Build Output Directory "web" - Project URL will be https://recipe.pages.dev
### Task 2: Create Cloudflare API Token (AC-6) - Navigate to Cloudflare Dashboard → My Profile → API Tokens - Create Custom Token with "Cloudflare Pages - Edit" permission - Configure Account Resources to include Recipe account - Copy token value (shown only once)
### Task 3: Find Cloudflare Account ID (AC-6) - Navigate to Cloudflare Dashboard → Account Home - Account ID visible in URL or sidebar (32-character hexadecimal string)
### Task 4: Configure GitHub Repository Secrets (AC-6) - Navigate to GitHub Repository → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions - Add CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN secret (paste token from Task 2) - Add CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID secret (paste account ID from Task 3)
### Task 5: Create GitHub Actions Workflow File (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3) - Create .github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml - Configure trigger: on push to main branch only - Setup steps: Checkout repository, Setup Go 1.24, Build WASM, Deploy to Cloudflare Pages - Use cloudflare/pages-action@v1 for deployment
### Task 6: Commit and Push Workflow (AC-1) - Add workflow file to Git - Commit with descriptive message - Push to main branch to trigger first deployment
### Task 7: Monitor First Deployment (AC-4, AC-5, AC-7) - Watch workflow execution in GitHub Actions tab - Check for errors in build/deploy steps - Verify deployment success and site accessibility at https://recipe.pages.dev - Test WASM conversion functionality end-to-end
### Task 8: Test Workflow Trigger Behavior (AC-1) - Test push to feature branch (should NOT trigger deployment) - Test push to main branch (should trigger deployment) - Test pull request behavior (should NOT trigger until merged)
### Task 9: Update README.md with Deployment Info (AC-5) - Add Deployment section documenting live URL and deployment process - Include manual deployment instructions and rollback procedure
### Task 10: Update sprint-status.yaml - Mark Story 7-5 as "drafted" in sprint-status.yaml - Preserve all comments and structure
### Task 11: Test Deployment Rollback (AC-7) - Create test deployment with trivial change - Access Cloudflare Pages Dashboard - Test rollback to previous deployment - Verify rollback completes successfully

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: GitHub Actions Workflow Triggers on Push to Main Branch - Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml - Trigger configuration: on push to branches [main] only - Workflow runs automatically on push to main (no manual trigger) - Does NOT run on pushes to other branches or on PR creation - Workflow run visible in GitHub Actions tab within 30 seconds
### AC-2: Workflow Builds WASM Binary - Go 1.24 toolchain setup using actions/setup-go@v5 - WASM build command: GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o web/recipe.wasm cmd/wasm/main.go - Build flags strip debug symbols to reduce binary size (~30%) - Build step completes without errors, web/recipe.wasm file created
### AC-3: Workflow Deploys web/ Directory to Cloudflare Pages - Uses cloudflare/pages-action@v1 for deployment - Deploys entire web/ folder (index.html, main.js, style.css, recipe.wasm) - Project name "recipe" matches Cloudflare Pages project - Authentication via CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID secrets - Deployment succeeds with no errors in logs
### AC-4: Deployment Completes in <5 Minutes - Total duration from workflow start to Cloudflare deployment success <5 minutes - Timeout configuration set to 10 minutes as safety net - Typical timing breakdown: Checkout <10s, Setup Go <1min, Build WASM <2min, Deploy <2min
### AC-5: Site is Accessible at https://recipe.pages.dev - Production URL accessible via HTTPS (enforced, HTTP redirects) - Site content loads: landing page, WASM binary, JavaScript, styles - Functionality works: drag-and-drop, format detection, conversion, file download - Performance: Initial load <3s, subsequent loads <500ms, global CDN latency <100ms
### AC-6: GitHub Repository Secrets Configured - Required secrets: CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID - Secrets configured in Repository → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions - API token has "Cloudflare Pages - Edit" permission - Secret values masked in workflow logs
### AC-7: Deployment Status Visible in GitHub Commit Status - Commit status indicator shows green checkmark (✓) for success, red X (✗) for failure - Click commit status icon links to workflow run details - Deployment URL visible if successful - Email notification sent on workflow failure

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Recipe provides three interfaces (CLI, TUI, Web) with privacy-first, client-side architecture. Cloudflare Pages hosts the static web interface with zero-cost global CDN deployment.

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification - Documentation & Deployment
doc.snippet: Cloudflare Pages deployment completes in <5 minutes from push to live. GitHub Actions workflow builds WASM binary and deploys web/ directory. Uses free tier (unlimited bandwidth, 500 builds/month).

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Cloudflare Pages integration: Deploys web/ directory, leverages automatic gzip compression (WASM reduced 70%), provides global CDN with sub-100ms latency worldwide. Zero-cost infrastructure using Cloudflare Pages and GitHub Actions free tiers.

doc.title: Story 7.1: Landing Page
doc.snippet: Landing page provides project description, 3-step usage guide, and privacy promise. Deployed to Cloudflare Pages via Story 7-5.

doc.title: Story 7.4: Legal Disclaimer
doc.snippet: Legal disclaimer section added to web/index.html covering reverse engineering disclosure and no-warranty statement. First deployment after Story 7-4 will include disclaimer on live site.

doc.title: Web Interface Documentation
doc.snippet: WASM build complete. Build command: GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -o web/static/recipe.wasm cmd/wasm/main.go. Web interface ready for Cloudflare Pages deployment.

artifact.reason: Existing WASM build target. GitHub Actions workflow will replicate this build command: GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -o web/recipe.wasm cmd/wasm/main.go with additional -ldflags="-s -w" for size optimization.

WASM entry point that exposes conversion functions to JavaScript. This file is compiled to web/recipe.wasm during GitHub Actions deployment workflow. Provides convert() function called by web/index.html.
Main landing page deployed to Cloudflare Pages. Includes hero section, drag-drop UI, privacy messaging, and WASM loading. This is the entry point users see at https://recipe.pages.dev.
Existing shell script for local WASM builds. GitHub Actions workflow will use similar commands but inline in workflow YAML rather than calling this script.
Currently exists in repository (pre-built). GitHub Actions workflow will rebuild this file on each deployment to ensure it matches latest code. Binary size target: <5MB compressed.

action.name: cloudflare/pages-action
action.purpose: Deploy web/ directory to Cloudflare Pages

service.name: Cloudflare Pages
service.purpose: Static hosting with global CDN (unlimited bandwidth, 500 builds/month)

story-context.constraints: - **Deployment Timing:** Total workflow execution must complete in <5 minutes (typical: 3-5 minutes). Timeout set to 10 minutes as safety net. - **Free Tier Limits:** Cloudflare Pages free tier provides 500 builds/month. GitHub Actions free tier provides unlimited minutes for public repositories on ubuntu runners. - **Zero Server Uploads:** All files processed client-side via WASM. Cloudflare Pages hosts only static files (HTML, JS, CSS, WASM binary). - **Build Flags:** WASM binary must be built with -ldflags="-s -w" to strip debug symbols and reduce size (~30% reduction). - **Security:** GitHub secrets (CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN, CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID) must be configured before workflow runs. Secret values automatically masked in workflow logs. - **Trigger Discipline:** Workflow must ONLY trigger on push to main branch, NOT on feature branch pushes or PR creation (to avoid consuming build quota unnecessarily). - **Project Name:** Cloudflare Pages project must be named "recipe" to match projectName in workflow configuration. - **Directory Structure:** Workflow deploys web/ directory. This must contain index.html, main.js, style.css, and recipe.wasm (built during workflow). - **Go Version:** Workflow uses Go 1.24 via actions/setup-go@v5. Must match project's go.mod version requirement. - **HTTPS Only:** Cloudflare Pages enforces HTTPS automatically. Production URL is https://recipe.pages.dev (HTTP redirects to HTTPS).

interface.name: GitHub Actions Workflow API
interface.signature: workflow: name: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages on: push: branches: [main] jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 10 steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-go@v5 with go-version: '1.24' - run: GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o web/recipe.wasm cmd/wasm/main.go - uses: cloudflare/pages-action@v1 with apiToken, accountId, projectName: recipe, directory: web

interface.name: Cloudflare Pages Action
interface.kind: GitHub Action for Cloudflare Pages deployment
interface.signature: inputs: apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }} # Required accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }} # Required projectName: recipe # Must match Cloudflare Pages project name directory: web # Directory to deploy (contains index.html, recipe.wasm, etc.) outputs: url: https://recipe.pages.dev # Deployment URL

interface.name: WASM Build Command
interface.signature:
GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o web/recipe.wasm cmd/wasm/main.go
Flags: GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm: Target WebAssembly platform -ldflags="-s -w": Strip debug symbols and DWARF info (reduces size ~30%) -o web/recipe.wasm: Output path (deployed to Cloudflare Pages) Input: cmd/wasm/main.go (WASM entry point) Output: web/recipe.wasm (compiled WASM binary, target size <5MB compressed)

interface.name: Cloudflare Pages Deployment
interface.signature: Project: recipe Production Branch: main Build Output Directory: web/ URL: https://recipe.pages.dev Features: - Global CDN (250+ data centers) - Automatic HTTPS with managed TLS certificates - Automatic gzip/brotli compression - Rollback capability (one-click in dashboard) - Preview deployments (optional, for PR testing)

tests.standards:
Epic 7 stories primarily involve documentation and CI/CD configuration with limited automated testing. Testing strategy for Story 7-5 focuses on manual verification and integration testing:
**Manual Testing:** - Workflow trigger verification (push to main triggers, push to feature branch does not) - WASM build success (check GitHub Actions logs for build completion) - Deployment success (verify Cloudflare Pages dashboard shows new deployment) - Site accessibility (visit https://recipe.pages.dev, verify page loads) - End-to-end functionality (upload file, convert, download - smoke test)
**GitHub Actions Testing:** - Secret validation (remove secrets, verify workflow fails with clear error) - Build failure handling (break cmd/wasm/main.go, verify workflow fails and commit status shows red X) - Timeout behavior (introduce delay, verify workflow times out at 10 minutes)
**Integration Testing:** - Full deployment flow from code push to live site - Rollback procedure (test in Cloudflare dashboard) - Performance measurement (deployment time <5 minutes)
**No automated unit tests** for workflow configuration (YAML syntax validation only).
tests.locations: - No automated test files for Story 7-5 (CI/CD configuration) - Manual testing checklist in story document (Task 7, Task 8, Task 11) - Workflow logs provide validation evidence (GitHub Actions → workflow run → logs)
tests.ideas:
### Test Ideas Mapped to Acceptance Criteria
**AC-1: GitHub Actions Workflow Triggers on Push to Main Branch** - TEST-1.1: Push commit to main branch → verify workflow appears in Actions tab within 30s - TEST-1.2: Push commit to feature branch → verify workflow does NOT trigger - TEST-1.3: Create PR from feature branch → verify workflow does NOT trigger on PR creation - TEST-1.4: Merge PR to main → verify workflow triggers after merge
**AC-2: Workflow Builds WASM Binary** - TEST-2.1: Check workflow logs for "Setup Go" step → verify Go 1.24 installed - TEST-2.2: Check workflow logs for "Build WASM" step → verify command executes successfully - TEST-2.3: Verify web/recipe.wasm mentioned in logs (file created) - TEST-2.4: Break cmd/wasm/main.go intentionally → verify build fails with error message **AC-3: Workflow Deploys web/ Directory to Cloudflare Pages** - TEST-3.1: Check workflow logs for "Deploy to Cloudflare Pages" step → verify deployment succeeds - TEST-3.2: Navigate to Cloudflare Pages dashboard → verify new deployment listed - TEST-3.3: Remove CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN secret → verify workflow fails with authentication error - TEST-3.4: Check deployed site → verify all files present (index.html, recipe.wasm, main.js, style.css)
**AC-4: Deployment Completes in <5 Minutes** - TEST-4.1: Measure total workflow duration from start to completion → target <5 minutes - TEST-4.2: Document timing breakdown (checkout, setup, build, deploy) from logs - TEST-4.3: Verify timeout configuration (workflow should fail if exceeds 10 minutes)
**AC-5: Site is Accessible at https://recipe.pages.dev** - TEST-5.1: Visit https://recipe.pages.dev → verify page loads (HTTP 200) - TEST-5.2: Verify HTTPS enforced (HTTP redirects to HTTPS) - TEST-5.3: Test conversion functionality → upload .xmp file, convert to .np3, download - TEST-5.4: Run Lighthouse audit → verify initial load <3 seconds
**AC-6: GitHub Repository Secrets Configured** - TEST-6.1: Navigate to Repository → Settings → Secrets → verify both secrets present - TEST-6.2: Check workflow logs → verify secret values masked (not visible in plain text) - TEST-6.3: Test workflow with valid secrets → verify deployment succeeds - TEST-6.4: Remove secrets → verify workflow fails with clear error message
**AC-7: Deployment Status Visible in GitHub Commit Status** - TEST-7.1: After successful deployment → verify green checkmark on commit page - TEST-7.2: Click commit status icon → verify links to workflow run - TEST-7.3: After failed deployment → verify red X on commit page - TEST-7.4: While deployment in progress → verify yellow dot displayed
